```skill
# Skill: Framework Strategy
TRIGGERS: "four primitives", "seven principles", "strategic assessment",
          "framework vision", "assumption test", "framework alignment",
          "HMW", "zone policy", "coordination overhead", "trust accumulation",
          "evidence gate", "environment contract", "context card", "observable stream"
ZONE: 0 (meta)
USED BY: Framework Owner agent
CONTEXT-TREE NODE: [FRAMEWORK OWNER]

## What this skill does
Carries the authoritative strategic knowledge of the Agentic Development Framework:
the four primitives, seven principles, open research questions, top assumptions,
and the assessment rubric used when reviewing agent artefacts.

## Load on activation
- references/four-primitives-and-principles.md
- references/assessment-rubric.md
- references/strategic-vision.md

## When to load this skill
LOAD FOR: any strategic advisory question about the framework
LOAD FOR: any artefact assessment — provides the rubric and the principles to assess against
LOAD FOR: any question about where the framework is going or what it is trying to prove

## When NOT to load this skill
NOT FOR: task-level or feature-level implementation decisions
NOT FOR: coding, styling, or infrastructure questions that belong to the stack

## Source documents
References in this skill are distilled from:
  .framework/meta/Agentic-Development-Framework.md      ← primitives, principles, theory
  .framework/meta/TopAssumptionsAfterEventStormingSession.md ← load-bearing assumptions
  .framework/meta/DecisionLog-20260228.md               ← committed design decisions
  .framework/meta/PolicyRegistry-v0.1.md                ← zone policy structure
  .framework/meta/Roadmap-v1.md                         ← sequencing and priorities
  .framework/meta/Context Engineering_ Our Approach.md  ← context engineering model

IF a meta document changes — update the corresponding reference file.
```
